theoremName: augmented pointwise repaired form nonnegative certificate
proofClass: interval/ball certificate with symbolic cone algebra
scope: For each fixed augmented trace-core index N only.  This certificate does not assert Mosco convergence, density, compactness, quotient compatibility, lower semicontinuity, or any finite-to-continuum promotion.

finiteSchurRepairInputStatus.label: finite Schur repair input
finiteSchurRepairInputStatus.reason: The interval/ball finite Schur consequence supplies D_aug,N>=0 and nonnegativity of the repaired finite Schur core form.

tailPositiveRestrictionInputStatus.label: positive Volterra tail restriction input
tailPositiveRestrictionInputStatus.reason: The positive Volterra tail theorem gives a nonnegative tail form; restricting a nonnegative form to a fixed core preserves nonnegativity.

fixedCoreConeSumStatus.label: fixed-core cone sum
fixedCoreConeSumStatus.reason: On each fixed core, q_N^rep is the sum of the finite Schur repaired core form and the restricted nonnegative Volterra tail form.

pointwiseRepairedFormNonnegativeStatus.label: pointwise repaired form nonnegative
pointwiseRepairedFormNonnegativeStatus.reason: A sum of two nonnegative quadratic forms is nonnegative.

exportedConsequence: For every fixed augmented trace-core index N, q_N^rep>=0.
proof: Import the finite augmented Schur repair consequence.
Restrict the nonnegative Volterra tail form to the same fixed core.
Use the exact fixed-core decomposition q_N^rep=q_N^Schur+t_N.
Apply the elementary cone rule: nonnegative plus nonnegative is nonnegative.
